# BTC Bridge
> An independent, non-custodial informational dashboard for understanding routes that move native Bitcoin liquidity to other blockchains or return tokenized Bitcoin to Bitcoin.

## What is BTC Bridge
BTC Bridge explains and compares technologies that connect Bitcoin with smart-contract networks. These systems commonly lock BTC and issue a redeemable token such as WBTC or tBTC, although some protocols instead exchange native assets through cross-chain liquidity. The dashboard does not custody funds, mint tokens, or guarantee routes; execution and custody assumptions belong to the selected protocol. Unlike a centralized exchange, a bridge normally settles through public blockchain transactions without requiring an exchange account.

## How it works
1. Select native BTC as the source and choose a destination network and Bitcoin representation.
2. Review the provider, receiving-token contract, exchange rate, fees, estimated confirmations, liquidity, and custody model.
3. The selected protocol supplies a Bitcoin deposit address or initiates a native cross-chain swap.
4. After the required Bitcoin confirmations, custodians, threshold signers, validators, or on-chain verification logic authorize delivery on the destination chain.
5. A reverse route generally burns or transfers the token and releases BTC to the user-supplied Bitcoin address.

## Key facts
- Bitcoin has no native, universal bridge standard; each bridge introduces its own verification and custody assumptions.
- Lock-and-mint, burn-and-release, liquidity-network, and native cross-chain swap designs are materially different.
- Ethereum Mainnet uses EIP-155 chain ID `1`; fungible Bitcoin representations there commonly implement ERC-20.
- Ethereum WBTC uses contract `0x2260FAC5E5542a773Aa44fBCfeDf7C193bc2C599`.
- Ethereum tBTC uses contract `0x18084fbA666a33D37592fA2633fD49a74DD93a88`.
- WBTC minting and redemption rely on authorized merchants and institutional custodians.
- tBTC secures deposited BTC through rotating signer groups and threshold cryptography under an honest-majority assumption.
- A non-custodial interface preserves control of wallet keys but does not remove bridge, signer, custodian, contract, liquidity, or token risks.
- Completion time depends on Bitcoin confirmations, destination finality, protocol processing, and congestion.

## Common questions
### Is BTC Bridge safe?
Safety depends on the chosen route, not the dashboard alone. Verify the provider domain, token contract, audits, custody model, destination address, and current protocol status before signing.

### Does the dashboard hold my BTC?
No. Any deposit or token approval is handled by the selected external protocol and the user’s wallets.

### What fees apply?
Possible costs include Bitcoin miner fees, destination gas, protocol charges, liquidity-provider fees, and swap slippage.

### How long does bridging take?
Transfers may take minutes or longer because providers wait for Bitcoin confirmations and may batch, sweep, attest, mint, or release funds afterward.

### Which assets and chains are supported?
Support is provider-specific. Confirm the exact token contract and network inside the selected protocol immediately before transacting.

### How do I start?
Use a self-custody Bitcoin wallet, a compatible destination wallet, and enough native gas currency on any chain requiring a later transaction.

### What are the main risks?
Key risks are compromised frontends, incorrect addresses, contract exploits, signer collusion, custodian failure, insufficient liquidity, depegging, delayed redemption, and irreversible transactions.

## App
- Dashboard: https://btc-bridge.github.io/
- App: https://btc-bridge.github.io/dashboard/

## Official resources
Ethereum Bridge Documentation: https://ethereum.org/developers/docs/bridges/
WBTC Documentation: https://docs.wbtc.network/
WBTC Proof of Reserves: https://docs.wbtc.network/overview/proof-of-reserve-and-transparency-dashboard
Threshold tBTC Documentation: https://docs.threshold.network/tbtc-v2
Threshold tBTC App: https://app.threshold.network/
tBTC Source Code: https://github.com/threshold-network/tbtc-v2
Threshold Security Audits: https://docs.threshold.network/resources/security-audits
tBTC Contract Addresses: https://docs.threshold.network/contract-addresses/tbtc

Bitcoin Explorer: https://mempool.space/
WBTC on Etherscan: https://etherscan.io/token/0x2260fac5e5542a773aa44fbcfedf7c193bc2c599
WBTC Market Data: https://www.coingecko.com/en/coins/wrapped-bitcoin
Bitcoin Bridge Protocol Data: https://defillama.com/protocols/bridge/bitcoin
Bridge Volume Data: https://defillama.com/bridges
ERC-20 Standard: https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-20
L2BEAT Assets and Bridges Framework: https://forum.l2beat.com/t/assets-bridges-and-tvs/388
